2022-04-01.title: Midnight Oil Brings Australia Home
2022-04-01.abstract: Australia has had its fair share of music that made it big overseas. But why do some bands succeed internationally while others do not? And does that even matter?

2022-04-01.title: After the Protesters Left, an Illicit Weed Began Growing in Parliament’s Garden
2022-04-01.abstract: New Zealand officials say anti-vaccination protesters seeded cannabis during a three-week occupation.

2022-04-01.title: Living With Gusto Despite a Rare, Fatal Disease, and Hunting for Answers
2022-04-01.abstract: At 26, Sammy Basso is believed to be the oldest person with the disease Progeria. And now he’s researching it, hoping to find a treatment and the secrets of aging.

2022-04-01.title: He Was Demoted for Harassing Women. Now He’s the Front-Runner for President.
2022-04-01.abstract: In Costa Rica’s runoff on Sunday, voters will decide between a candidate found to have sexually harassed junior employees at the World Bank and a former president once accused of corruption.

2022-04-01.title: A nuclear agency is investigating reports of radiation poisoning among Russian troops at Chernobyl.
2022-04-01.abstract: Russian soldiers left the decommissioned nuclear plant and a nearby city after five weeks of occupation.

2022-04-02.title: Two high schools in New Jersey reinstate mask mandates following outbreaks.
2022-04-02.abstract: New coronavirus cases have led to the resumption, if temporarily, of some mitigation strategies.

2022-04-02.title: How Kyiv Has Withstood Russia’s Attacks
2022-04-02.abstract: Russia has more troops and more firepower. Why has it failed to seize the capital?

2022-04-02.title: Yemen’s Warring Parties Begin First Cease-Fire in 6 Years
2022-04-02.abstract: The two-month truce, brokered by the United Nations, offers hope for a reduction of violence in a war that has rattled the Arabian Peninsula and caused a humanitarian crisis.

2022-04-02.title: How Catholics Avoided Paying Millions in Reparations for Residential Schools
2022-04-02.abstract: During its final weeks in office, the previous Conservative government reached a deal allowing the church to walk away from most of its obligations.

2022-04-02.title: Israeli Troops Kill 3 Palestinian Militants in West Bank After Rise in Attacks
2022-04-02.abstract: The Israeli authorities said the men were killed in a shootout. The security forces have ramped up their operations after a wave of violence.

2022-04-03.title: ‘Macbeth’ performances on Broadway pause after Daniel Craig tests positive for the coronavirus.
2022-04-03.abstract: The show, which just began previews on Tuesday, hopes to resume on April 8.

2022-04-03.title: Why is the prime minister in trouble?
2022-04-03.abstract: Pakistan has been gripped by a political crisis after Prime Minister Imran Khan appeared to lose support from the military and a coalition of opposition parties moved to vote him out of office.

2022-04-03.title: ‘This Is True Barbarity’: Life and Death Under Russian Occupation
2022-04-03.abstract: The town of Trostyanets was occupied by Russian forces for a month before the Ukrainian military liberated it. Residents described weeks of hunger and horror.

2022-04-03.title: Who is Imran Khan?
2022-04-03.abstract: He was once a national hero who led Pakistan’s cricket team to a World Cup victory. Now, the embattled prime minister is fighting to stay in power.

2022-04-03.title: Taliban Outlaw Opium Poppy Cultivation in Afghanistan
2022-04-03.abstract: The move will have far-reaching consequences for the many farmers who turned to the illicit crop as a brutal drought and economic crisis have gripped the country.

2022-04-04.title: With Each New Crisis in Hong Kong, Pressure Built on a Beijing Loyalist
2022-04-04.abstract: Carrie Lam, the territory’s chief executive, said Monday that she would not seek a second term. Her tenure was marked by protests, a contentious law and a devastating Covid outbreak.

2022-04-04.title: French Election Opens Up as Marine Le Pen Surges
2022-04-04.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron’s belated entry into the campaign and his focus on Ukraine have left him vulnerable to a strong challenge from the right.

2022-04-04.title: Bristling Against the West, China Rallies Domestic Sympathy for Russia
2022-04-04.abstract: China’s Communist Party is mounting an ideological campaign aimed at officials and students. The message: The country will not turn its back on Russia.

2022-04-04.title: Zelensky blasts Merkel and Sarkozy for denying Ukraine NATO membership.
2022-04-04.abstract: In a video address, he said that NATO’s decision in 2008 not to give Ukraine a path to membership helped lead to atrocities by Russian forces.

2022-04-04.title: Carrie Lam, racked by Covid failures, won’t seek a new term to lead Hong Kong.
2022-04-04.abstract: HONG KONG — Carrie Lam, the chief executive of Hong Kong, announced on Monday that she would not seek a second term, after a huge surge in coronavirus infections left the global financial hub with one of the highest virus death rates in the world.

2022-04-05.title: Up-Close Ukraine Atrocity Photographs Touch a Global Nerve
2022-04-05.abstract: The apparent execution of Ukrainian civilians by retreating Russian forces, their bodies strewn in streets and yards, has focused attention on what constitutes crimes in war.

2022-04-05.title: 200,000 Dead, One Lone Defendant as Darfur Trial Begins
2022-04-05.abstract: A brutal campaign in western Sudan left the world aghast. Two decades later, the first and only Darfur war crimes trial opened for a man accused of leading the terrifying janjaweed militia.

2022-04-05.title: What are war crimes, and how are they prosecuted?
2022-04-05.abstract: World leaders have vowed to hold President Vladimir V. Putin responsible for any atrocities by Russian forces. Past experience shows such cases take years to build and prosecute.

2022-04-05.title: The U.N. Security Council meets as Ukraine accuses Russia of atrocities.
2022-04-05.abstract: Mr. Zelensky was expected to make the case that Russian forces have committed war crimes, as evidence emerges of civilian killings in the suburbs of Kyiv.

2022-04-05.title: For Macron, France’s Troubled Industries Hit Home
2022-04-05.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron vowed an economic revival, but as he seeks re-election, a Potemkin factory in the town where he was raised shows just how hard that can be.

2022-04-06.title: El Salvador’s New Law on Gangs Raises Censorship Fears
2022-04-06.abstract: A measure punishing the sharing of information about gangs is so vague, critics say, that virtually anyone can be arrested for speaking or writing about them, putting journalists in the cross hairs.

2022-04-06.title: Amid talk of war crimes in Ukraine, there are lessons from the Balkan bloodshed.
2022-04-06.abstract: The indictment of Slobodan Milosevic, the president of Yugoslavia, was a seminal moment in legal history. But prosecuting war crimes remains a steep climb.

2022-04-06.title: Why Tracking Putin’s Wealth Is So Difficult
2022-04-06.abstract: Amid speculation that oligarchs are holding cash and luxury assets for the Russian president, many of his extravagances can be traced elsewhere: the Russian state.

2022-04-06.title: The End of the (Pipe)line? Germany Scrambles to Wean Itself Off Russian Gas
2022-04-06.abstract: Dangerously dependent on Russian gas, Germany is still refusing to cut off President Putin, whose war it is effectively subsidizing to the tune of some $220 million a day.

2022-04-06.title: Israel’s Government in Crisis After Senior Lawmaker Quits Coalition
2022-04-06.abstract: Idit Silman, the de facto government whip, left the coalition, depriving the government of a parliamentary majority. But her move does not mean the opposition can force new elections, at least for now.

2022-04-07.title: The W.H.O. warns against relaxing restrictions too quickly in Latin America.
2022-04-07.abstract: World Health Organization officials on Wednesday expressed concerns that some countries in the Americas were prematurely scaling back policies to control the coronavirus, and urged them to double down on efforts to increase vaccination and testing as cases remain high in Europe.

2022-04-07.title: In his nightly address, Zelensky challenged allies to find the determination to ban Russian oil.
2022-04-07.abstract: He also accused Russian troops of starting to hide evidence of civilian deaths.

2022-04-07.title: A leading provider of testing in Arizona is suspending 60 sites.
2022-04-07.abstract: Embry Health, a leading coronavirus testing provider in Arizona, said it would suspend operations at 60 sites in the state and would no longer offer free tests for uninsured people, joining testing companies across the United States that have changed policies after the federal government said it could no longer reimburse providers for caring for Covid patients without insurance.

2022-04-07.title: On the Scene: At the Lviv Train Station
2022-04-07.abstract: Lviv is on the outer edges of the war, but at the city's train station you can see the turmoil, between people escaping and others returning home.

2022-04-07.title: Ukraine’s Eurovision entry goes on tour to help fund the war effort.
2022-04-07.abstract: Kalush Orchestra, a group that mixes rap and traditional Ukrainian music, was given special permission to leave the country and play internationally.

2022-04-08.title: Pink Floyd says it will release its first new track in decades to support Ukraine.
2022-04-08.abstract: The song, “Hey Hey Rise Up,” is a reworking of a protest song from World War I and features the Ukrainian rock star Andriy Khlyvnyuk of the band Boombox.

2022-04-08.title: Deadly Shooting in Tel Aviv
2022-04-08.abstract: The attack occurred outside a bar filled with people on the last night of the workweek. It was the fourth lethal attack in Israel in less than three weeks.

2022-04-08.title: For Ukrainian Writer, War Evokes Scars of Time in Captivity
2022-04-08.abstract: Stanislav Aseyev, a 32-year-old journalist, had documented his abuse in a prison run by Russian-backed separatists. Now, the war reminds him of why Ukrainians are fighting for their lives.

2022-04-08.title: The Biggest Promoter of Mexico’s Presidential Recall Election? The President.
2022-04-08.abstract: The vote has the potential to upend the country’s political system. But many fear it will amount to nothing more than a tool for propaganda.

2022-04-08.title: ‘La French Tech’ Arrives Under Macron, but Proves No Panacea
2022-04-08.abstract: The president has brought innovation, jobs and growth. Still, resentments fester on the eve of the presidential election.

2022-04-09.title: The BA.2 subvariant prompts a slight increase in new U.S. cases.
2022-04-09.abstract: The daily average of new coronavirus cases in the United States ticked up 1 percent on Friday as BA.2, the highly transmissible Omicron subvariant, continued its rapid spread. It was the first uptick after weeks of recovery from the Omicron-fueled winter surge.

2022-04-09.title: Saudi Arabia will allow a million hajj pilgrims to visit Mecca, with Covid precautions.
2022-04-09.abstract: Vaccinations and negative tests will be required for those who attend the annual ritual.

2022-04-09.title: Japan dials up measures against Russia, aligning itself with the U.S. and Europe.
2022-04-09.abstract: The country has announced a ban on Russian coal and restrictions on imports including timber, vodka and machinery. It is also expelling eight Russian diplomats.

2022-04-09.title: Spurred by Putin, Russians Turn on One Another Over the War
2022-04-09.abstract: Citizens are denouncing one another, illustrating how the war is feeding paranoia and polarization in Russian society.

2022-04-09.title: With Macron and Le Pen Leading Election Field, a Fractured France Decides
2022-04-09.abstract: In Dijon, magnificence and malaise sit side by side, in the image of a country divided before the presidential vote on Sunday.

2022-04-10.title: Why Calls for War Crimes Justice Over Ukraine Face Long Odds
2022-04-10.abstract: If those in power act as if they are immune to the laws of war, it may be because they often are. But following through is not necessarily an empty exercise.

2022-04-10.title: An Australia Homecoming, Mixed With Yearning and Trepidation
2022-04-10.abstract: When “Fortress Australia” sealed its borders, thousands of citizens were stuck abroad. When allowed to return home, a reporter wondered how she would find the country — and how it would find her.

2022-04-10.title: A Reporter Returns to Australia, a Pandemic Later
2022-04-10.abstract: Earlier this year, I boarded a plane home for the first time since the pandemic began.

2022-04-10.title: Macron’s pursuit of a high-tech economy stirs pre-election resentment.
2022-04-10.abstract: A start-up boom in France was a core objective for Emmanuel Macron, a young leader elected president five years ago as a restless disrupter, promising to pry open the economy and make it competitive in the 21st century.

2022-04-10.title: A Popular British Politician Falls From Grace Over a Tax Scandal
2022-04-10.abstract: Rishi Sunak was once considered a potential successor to Prime Minister Boris Johnson. Now he faces scrutiny over his wealthy wife’s tax status, and over holding an American green card while in office.

2022-04-11.title: ‘Cheap as Chips’ No Longer True in U.K. as Prices Soar for a Favorite Meal
2022-04-11.abstract: The costs for fish and chips, a working-class staple in Britain for well over a century, have rocketed as the war in Ukraine has made the main ingredients scarcer. Of 10,000 shops, 3,000 may close.

2022-04-11.title: Bucha’s Month of Terror
2022-04-11.abstract: We documented dozens of killings of civilians and interviewed scores of witnesses to uncover Russian atrocities in Ukraine.

2022-04-11.title: Shehbaz Sharif Becomes Pakistan’s New Prime Minister Amid Turmoil
2022-04-11.abstract: Mr. Sharif, whose family has been dogged by accusations of graft for decades, was selected by Pakistan’s Parliament at a time of deep political divisions and economic tumult.

2022-04-11.title: With Threats All Around, Japan Moves to Shed Its Pacifist Constraints
2022-04-11.abstract: Growing calls for more military spending after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine demonstrate Japan’s recognition that it must bolster its own deterrent power, not just rely on its U.S. alliance.

2022-04-11.title: Despite Low Turnout, Mexico Voters Back President to Stay in Office
2022-04-11.abstract: President Andrés Manuel López Obrador drew overwhelming support among those who cast ballots, but the recall referendum failed to achieve the required 40 percent threshold to be binding.

2022-04-12.title: Name, phone, address: The writing on a child’s back is now a symbol of Ukrainian parents’ terror.
2022-04-12.abstract: A mother who wrote emergency contact information on her 2-year-old’s back has become a voice for countless Ukrainian parents forced to do the same.

2022-04-12.title: From the U.S. to China: A 3-Month Quarantine Horror Story
2022-04-12.abstract: A lawyer flew home to China hoping to see his family for the first time since the pandemic began. Instead, he was trapped in three months of quarantine.

2022-04-12.title: This Beach in Mexico Is an L.G.B.T.Q. Haven. But Can It Last?
2022-04-12.abstract: The town of Zipolite has become an oasis for the queer community. But as its popularity has surged, some worry that the town’s spirit is beginning to vanish.

2022-04-12.title: An Angry Public Wants Sri Lanka’s President Gone
2022-04-12.abstract: Gotabaya Rajapaksa is used to silencing and discrediting his political opponents. But that’s harder to do this time.

2022-04-12.title: Boris Johnson Fined for Breaking U.K. Lockdown Rules
2022-04-12.abstract: The British prime minister had seemed to turn the “partygate” tide with his handling of Ukraine. Then came the announcement that he broke the law.

2022-04-13.title: President Biden accuses Russia of committing genocide in Ukraine.
2022-04-13.abstract: It was the first time the president has used the term to describe the Russia’s war. He emphasized it was a personal view, not a legal determination.

2022-04-13.title: Pencil Cases and Air-Raid Sirens: School at War for Ukraine’s Children
2022-04-13.abstract: The country’s teachers are doing their best to provide lessons for the millions of school-age children whose education has been interrupted.

2022-04-13.title: The Philippines Toppled One Marcos. Now His Son May Become President.
2022-04-13.abstract: Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has spent his political career trying to rehabilitate the family name. As the front-runner in the upcoming election, he may finally succeed.

2022-04-13.title: More Than 120 Dead After Tropical Storm Hits the Philippines
2022-04-13.abstract: The first such storm of the year caused widespread flooding and landslides in the central part of the country.

2022-04-13.title: New Zealand welcomes vaccinated tourists from Australia and relaxes more policies.
2022-04-13.abstract: The latest steps toward reopening are being taken in a country that has maintained some of the strictest coronavirus precautions in the world.

2022-04-14.title: The U.S. is considering sending a high-level official to Kyiv.
2022-04-14.abstract: No American official has publicly visited Ukraine since Russia launched its invasion in late February.

2022-04-14.title: A Russian warship is ‘seriously damaged’ in the Black Sea. Conflicting explanations emerge.
2022-04-14.abstract: Ukraine claimed it was the same vessel that was famously and obscenely told off by Ukrainian troops in February.

2022-04-14.title: ‘Down With France’: Former Colonies in Africa Demand a Reset
2022-04-14.abstract: Decades after independence, many African countries are increasingly troubled by the ongoing influence of their former colonial power.

2022-04-14.title: Hiding in Plain Sight, a Soviet-Era Air Defense System Arrives in Ukraine
2022-04-14.abstract: Encouraged by the United States, Slovakia “donated” a bulky but effective S-300, hoping to challenge Russian domination of the skies in the war next door.

2022-04-14.title: U.K. Plans to Send Some Asylum Seekers to Rwanda
2022-04-14.abstract: Britain says it will fly many refugees thousands of miles to the central African country for processing and resettlement, a significant hardening of migration policy.

2022-04-15.title: A reporter covering the war reflects on the legacy of the Holocaust.
2022-04-15.abstract: In eastern Poland, perceptions of Russia and Ukraine are shaped by a traumatic past.

2022-04-15.title: Kim Jong-un Gives North Korean TV Anchor a Luxury Home
2022-04-15.abstract: Ri Chun-hee, who delivers the news with a soaring, bombastic tenor, received a two-story apartment in a riverside complex from the country’s leader, apparently for her loyalty.

2022-04-15.title: Journey to Transnistria: Inside Russia’s Disinformation Bubble
2022-04-15.abstract: We traveled inside Transnistria, a Moscow-backed breakaway state in Moldova, to find out how Russia’s disinformation campaign stretches beyond its borders.

2022-04-15.title: Faced With a Changed Europe, China Sticks to an Old Script
2022-04-15.abstract: Europe was viewed as lacking the power and the will to contest China’s rise. But now, a harder line toward Russia has implications for Beijing.

2022-04-15.title: Tanzania’s First Female President Wants to Bring Her Nation in From the Cold
2022-04-15.abstract: Samia Suluhu Hassan became the only female head of government in Africa when her predecessor suddenly died. She is setting a new course.

2022-04-16.title: As Russia Pivots East, Ukrainians Ask: Are We Next?
2022-04-16.abstract: Our cameras captured life on the front lines in southeast Ukraine, where Russian shelling is relentless and it feels like the war is inching closer every day.

2022-04-16.title: Putin’s Ukraine Gamble Pivots to a Very Different Battlefield
2022-04-16.abstract: After Russian forces were mauled in cities and towns in northern Ukraine, Vladimir V. Putin is shifting the focus of his invasion to the flatlands of Donbas, in the east.

2022-04-16.title: In Quebec, the Clash Over Caribou Heats Up
2022-04-16.abstract: Canada is threatening to use, for the first time, a provision in endangered species legislation to intervene and force caribou habitat protection in Quebec.

2022-04-16.title: Where Russians Turn for Uncensored News on Ukraine
2022-04-16.abstract: Telegram is the platform of choice for Russians seeking to escape Moscow’s propaganda web. But can it last?

2022-04-16.title: War in Ukraine Has Left Eastern Europe Sleepless
2022-04-16.abstract: Mental health professionals across the region say they have seen an increase in patients with intense anxiety. Some are asking for more sleeping pills, and many are making escape plans.

2022-04-17.title: North Korea Launches 2 Short-Range Missiles
2022-04-17.abstract: The missile test, the North’s 12th of the year, took place as South Korea and the United States were preparing to begin joint military exercises.

2022-04-17.title: Israeli Government Crisis Deepens After Closing of Major Mosque
2022-04-17.abstract: An Islamist party said it was freezing its involvement in the coalition government after the Israeli police blocked Muslim access to the Aqsa Mosque compound to prevent clashes.

2022-04-17.title: Fighting for Her Life, Far From Ukraine
2022-04-17.abstract: A 5-year-old Ukrainian girl with a brain tumor was one of several children brought for treatment in the United States after their country was invaded by Russia.

2022-04-17.title: The World’s Broken Promise of Asylum
2022-04-17.abstract: Britain’s new plan to send thousands of asylum seekers to Rwanda exposes the fragile nature of the global refugee compact, which has always fallen prey to domestic politics.

2022-04-17.title: Rare Overlap of Holy Days Shows Jerusalem’s Promise and Problems
2022-04-17.abstract: In its Old City, a Christian, a Jew and a Muslim marked Easter, Passover and Ramadan. To some, it’s a “symphony.” To others, a reminder of division.

2022-04-18.title: French Candidates’ Economic Programs Hold Key to the Election
2022-04-18.abstract: Promising tax cuts, higher wages and changes in the retirement age, President Macron and Marine Le Pen vie for undecided voters.

2022-04-18.title: A ‘Wild West’ of Marijuana Shops Grows in Toronto
2022-04-18.abstract: Permitted to operate during the pandemic, even during lockdowns, Toronto’s marijuana shops have flourished and changed the character of an iconic neighborhood.

2022-04-18.title: Russian attack on Lviv shakes sense of security in a relatively safe city.
2022-04-18.abstract: Vlada Belau believed she had finally established a semblance of normalcy in the city of Lviv after fleeing from Kyiv, nearly 300 miles to the east, when it was bombarded in the early days of the war.

2022-04-18.title: To Push Back Russians, Ukrainians Hit a Village With Cluster Munitions
2022-04-18.abstract: The New York Times verified that Ukraine’s forces appeared to fire at least two of these internationally banned weapons in a neighborhood, putting Ukrainian civilians at extreme risk.

2022-04-18.title: A Russian strike hits Lviv, a city relatively untouched by violence.
2022-04-18.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of displaced people from other parts of Ukraine have fled to, or through, Lviv to escape the fighting. Then it landed on their doorstep.

2022-04-19.title: The U.N.’s humanitarian chief said he tested positive for the coronavirus after attending in-person meetings.
2022-04-19.abstract: One meeting had included the U.N.’s secretary general, but everyone in the room was wearing masks, a spokesman said.

2022-04-19.title: Myanmar’s Health System Is in Collapse, ‘Obliterated’ by the Regime
2022-04-19.abstract: The country is also now one of the most dangerous places in the world to be a medical worker. At least 30 doctors have been killed since the coup, a rights group says.

2022-04-19.title: For passengers in midflight on Monday, there were cheers and alarm as a U.S. mask mandate ended.
2022-04-19.abstract: Airport terminals and flights erupted in applause, while some passengers said the abrupt policy change put them at risk.

2022-04-19.title: China’s Covid shutdowns go far beyond Shanghai.
2022-04-19.abstract: Cities and towns across the country are shutting down, hoping to sidestep the pitfalls and stumbles that Shanghai has faced battling the Omicron variant.

2022-04-19.title: Russia’s Thrust in Eastern Ukraine Combines Firepower and New Caution
2022-04-19.abstract: The military campaign that Moscow said it had now begun appears much more methodical than those it pursued in the war’s first days

2022-04-20.title: Russia rejects calls for a cease-fire to enable evacuations, saying Ukraine only wants time to arm.
2022-04-20.abstract: Civilians, including children, remain trapped in the eastern Ukraine region of Donbas and in the devastated port city of Mariupol.

2022-04-20.title: Shanghai’s Low Covid Death Toll Revives Questions About China’s Numbers
2022-04-20.abstract: The country’s largest city has recorded just 17 Covid deaths, despite surging cases. How China defines a Covid death may be part of the reason.

2022-04-20.title: Deep in Colombia, Rebels and Soldiers Fight for the Same Prize: Drugs
2022-04-20.abstract: The country signed a historic peace deal more than five years ago. But a power vacuum is fueling the rise of new armed groups competing to control the drug trade.

2022-04-20.title: 6 Rohingya Refugees Are Killed Fleeing Detention Center in Malaysia
2022-04-20.abstract: In recent years, the Malaysian government has taken a more hard-line approach to the refugees in the country, which once offered a modicum of opportunity.

2022-04-20.title: In the fog of dementia, one grandmother learns again and again that her country is at war.
2022-04-20.abstract: Millions of Ukrainians have been displaced since the war started. Many elderly or disabled people have been unable or unwilling to leave their homes.

2022-04-21.title: See Mariupol’s Azovstal steel-mill fortress in peacetime, and then after nearly two months of war.
2022-04-21.abstract: Before-and-after images show the scale of destruction that the Ukrainian port city has suffered after nearly two months of near constant bombardment by Russian artillery, mortars and airstrikes.

2022-04-21.title: Macron and Le Pen Trade Punches in Pivotal Debate on France’s Future
2022-04-21.abstract: He attacked the far-right leader as a Putin stooge. She hit back at him as the president of division and contempt.

2022-04-21.title: Why Are Kangaroos Turning Up in India?
2022-04-21.abstract: The animals are the latest exotic fauna to be smuggled into the country, possibly to be used as pets. Draft legislation would close loopholes in the roaring wildlife trade.

2022-04-21.title: Israel and Gaza Militants Exchange Fire in Worst Fighting in Months
2022-04-21.abstract: The flare-up followed a rise in tensions over holy places in Jerusalem and a deadly wave of Arab attacks in Israel, answered by a lethal Israeli crackdown in the occupied West Bank.

2022-04-21.title: Welcome to a Village With More Booksellers Than School Pupils
2022-04-21.abstract: Urueña, in northwestern Spain, has fought depopulation by reinventing itself as a literary hub. The full-time population is still just 100, but there are 11 shops selling books.

2022-04-22.title: Ukraine needs up to $7 billion in support per month, Zelensky says.
2022-04-22.abstract: Ukraine has suffered $550 billion in losses from the war, President Volodymyr Zelensky told the World Bank on Thursday.

2022-04-22.title: Aboard the Star Ferry in Hong Kong
2022-04-22.abstract: The Star Ferry in Hong Kong has been offering an exhilarating ride for 142 years. Experience a trip with a Times reporter.

2022-04-22.title: When Will the Tourists Be Back?
2022-04-22.abstract: An industry weathering a long winter looks warily ahead.

2022-04-22.title: Departing South Korean Leader Exchanges Farewell Letters With Kim Jong-un
2022-04-22.abstract: President Moon Jae-in, who is barred from seeking re-election after his five-year term, and the North’s leader had warm parting words amid a diplomatic stalemate.

2022-04-22.title: Le Pen Closer Than Ever to the French Presidency (and to Putin)
2022-04-22.abstract: As elections approach Sunday, the far-right candidate is linked to the Russian president by a web of financial ties and a history of support that has hardly dimmed despite the war in Ukraine.

2022-04-23.title: Rattled by Ukraine War, Moldova Strives to Dodge Russia’s Long Shadow
2022-04-23.abstract: Moldova’s destiny has been intertwined uncomfortably with Russia’s for years, and the Ukraine invasion has accelerated its efforts to break free from Moscow.

2022-04-23.title: Canada’s Stanley Cup Hopes Rest on Leafs, Flames, Oilers
2022-04-23.abstract: The N.H.L. playoffs are a beloved routine of Canadian springtime that can make fans as crazy as the season’s mercurial weather.

2022-04-23.title: Where is Transnistria, and why is it relevant to the war in Ukraine?
2022-04-23.abstract: A Russian general said that gaining control of southern Ukraine would give Moscow “yet another point of access” to the breakaway republic in eastern Moldova.

2022-04-23.title: Discreetly, the Young in Japan Chip Away at a Taboo on Tattoos
2022-04-23.abstract: Exposed to body art on social media, more young Japanese are getting tattoos, even if that means hiding them at work.

2022-04-23.title: Warsaw’s Welcome Mat Risks Fraying Under Strain of a New Refugee Surge
2022-04-23.abstract: Warsaw gladly and proudly accepted 300,000 Ukrainians fleeing the war. But as the Polish capital braces for a new wave of refugees, its mayor warns that the city is “at capacity.”

2022-04-24.title: ‘The Daily’ explores what’s at stake in the runoff election.
2022-04-24.abstract: Voters in France are deciding between the same two candidates as the last presidential election: Emmanuel Macron, the president and a polished centrist, and Marine Le Pen, the leader of the far-right National Rally party. But the context this time around is different from that 2017 vote. There is a war in Europe, and the contest is tight. “The Daily” podcast looks at the stakes, and how the race has become so close.

2022-04-24.title: Macron and Le Pen spar in an increasingly divided France.
2022-04-24.abstract: PARIS — In the course of a visit this past week to Saint-Denis, north of Paris, where the poverty rate is about twice the national average, President Emmanuel Macron donned boxing gloves for a moment to spar with a local. “Go on, hit me,” the young man said, “show me what you got!”

2022-04-24.title: At Least 10 Dead After Tour Boat Sinks in Japan
2022-04-24.abstract: Sixteen people were still missing, more than 24 hours after the sightseeing vessel was lost off the coast of Hokkaido island.

2022-04-24.title: The French presidential voting system is simple, but also complex.
2022-04-24.abstract: A candidate who gets an absolute majority of votes in the first round is elected outright, an unlikely outcome that has never come to pass since the French started choosing presidents by direct popular vote in 1965.

2022-04-24.title: A victory for Le Pen would be a debacle for the E.U.
2022-04-24.abstract: BRUSSELS — Should Marine Le Pen prove the polls wrong and win the French presidency on Sunday, she would immediately represent a severe danger to both the European Union and NATO.

2022-04-25.title: Biden nominates an ambassador in Kyiv, aiming to fill a void in the U.S.-Ukraine relationship.
2022-04-25.abstract: President Biden nominated Bridget Brink, the current U.S. ambassador to Slovakia, as ambassador to Ukraine. The position has been empty for more than a year.

2022-04-25.title: Covid Outbreak in Beijing Prompts Order for Nearly Citywide Testing
2022-04-25.abstract: Supermarkets stocked up as panic buying began. Municipal authorities ordered that almost all residents be tested three times this week to contain a rising number of cases.

2022-04-25.title: Their cover blown, Blinken and Austin pushed on to Kyiv despite the security risks.
2022-04-25.abstract: After President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine spilled the secret that the secretaries of state and defense would visit, the two cabinet members decided to press onward regardless.

2022-04-25.title: How Zelensky Tamed Ukraine’s Fractious Politics and Stood Up to Putin
2022-04-25.abstract: Ukrainian politics were known for sharp-elbowed infighting. But as he defends his country against the Russian invasion, President Volodymyr Zelensky has his government presenting a unified front.

2022-04-25.title: Four People Fatally Stabbed in London
2022-04-25.abstract: A suspect was in custody Monday morning, and the police say they believe the attacker and the victims knew each other.

2022-04-26.title: A Hong Kong Actress Wears Brownface. Cue Outrage and Shrugs.
2022-04-26.abstract: The TV show “Barrack O’Karma 1968” fueled debate online. To many Filipinos, it was about racism and classism. Other viewers jumped to the actress’s defense.

2022-04-26.title: Kenneth Roth, ‘Godfather’ of Human Rights Work, to Step Down
2022-04-26.abstract: Mr. Roth joined Human Rights Watch when it was a fledgling organization. It has expanded into one of the most influential human rights advocacy groups.

2022-04-26.title: Baking Challah in Dubai: A Jewish Community Heads Out Into the Open
2022-04-26.abstract: The increasing openness of Jewish life in the Persian Gulf emirate of Dubai is another sign of an emerging new reality in the Middle East, where Israel’s isolation by the Arab world is ebbing.

2022-04-26.title: North Korea Holds Military Parade With Nuclear Threat
2022-04-26.abstract: In a speech, Kim Jong-un, the country’s leader, vowed to expand his nuclear arsenal ​“at the fastest possible speed​.”

2022-04-26.title: Beijing Starts Testing Most Residents to Try to Avoid a Lockdown Like Shanghai’s
2022-04-26.abstract: China’s capital reported 22 cases as it kicked off an ambitious mandatory testing campaign across the city and reassured residents that food and other supplies were plentiful.

2022-04-27.title: Americans’ support for admitting refugees is at a record high, Gallup finds.
2022-04-27.abstract: Gallup reported the record high after its new poll found that 78 percent of adults in the United States approve of allowing up to 100,000 Ukrainian refugees into the country.

2022-04-27.title: Aung San Suu Kyi Found Guilty in Myanmar Ahead of Biden Summit
2022-04-27.abstract: The elected civilian leader, who was detained in a military coup last year, was sentenced to five years in prison in a corruption trial that was closed to the public.

2022-04-27.title: Singapore Executes Drug Smuggler Despite Concerns Over Mental Disability
2022-04-27.abstract: Defense lawyers had argued that the Malaysian man should not be executed because he was not fully capable of understanding his actions and had been coerced.

2022-04-27.title: European gas prices jump after Gazprom cuts natural gas to Poland and Bulgaria.
2022-04-27.abstract: The market reaction underscored that a dreaded moment in the war had arrived: the severe disruption of Russian natural gas exports to the E.U.

2022-04-27.title: China’s Covid Lockdown Outrage Tests Limits of Triumphant Propaganda
2022-04-27.abstract: Public anger and grief over the bungled lockdown in Shanghai is creating a credibility crisis for the country’s leader, Xi Jinping, and his zero Covid policies.

2022-04-28.title: In El Salvador, the President Cracks Down on Civil Liberties, and Is Beloved for It
2022-04-28.abstract: Much of the country’s population is willing to tolerate an autocratic leader, if it means that someone will finally solve their most pressing problem: gang violence.

2022-04-28.title: The Extreme Heat Pummeling India and Pakistan Is About to Get Worse
2022-04-28.abstract: The Indian subcontinent has recorded above-average temperatures for weeks. Heat-related weather watches or alerts are now in effect for hundreds of millions of people.

2022-04-28.title: Touring wreckage near Kyiv, the top U.N. official declares the war ‘an absurdity.’
2022-04-28.abstract: António Guterres’ goals include securing evacuation routes for besieged civilians and supporting the prosecution of war crimes.

2022-04-28.title: South Korea Arrests 2 Accused of Spying for North Korea
2022-04-28.abstract: The men were paid in cryptocurrency by a man suspected of being a North Korean spy agent, the South Korean national police agency said.

2022-04-28.title: Tensions Flare Between Neighbors After Afghan Man Kills Iranian Clerics
2022-04-28.abstract: The attack led to ethnic tensions in both countries, which each sent troops to the border.

2022-04-29.title: Russian intelligence was behind an attack on the editor of Novaya Gazeta, a U.S. official says.
2022-04-29.abstract: Dmitri Muratov was on a train preparing to leave Moscow at the time of the attack, which caused chemical burns to his eyes.

2022-04-29.title: Is Australian Democracy in Decline?
2022-04-29.abstract: With a federal election just a few weeks away, it’s time to put Australia’s system and political flaws into context.

2022-04-29.title: 8,000 British troops will join allied exercises in Europe, officials say.
2022-04-29.abstract: The troops will serve in the British-led Joint Expeditionary Force, created in 2014 in response to Russia’s seizure of Crimea from Ukraine.

2022-04-29.title: They Survived the Holocaust. Now, They Are Fleeing to Germany.
2022-04-29.abstract: A complex operation is underway to evacuate Holocaust survivors from Ukraine. For rescuers and evacuees alike, it means confronting the past.

2022-04-29.title: On Ireland’s Census, a Blank Box Gives Residents a Chance to Tell Their Stories
2022-04-29.abstract: The makers of the survey included a space for people to write or draw a message. The contents of these so-called time capsules will be revealed in 100 years.

2022-04-30.title: The death of an American fighting for Ukraine is confirmed by his family.
2022-04-30.abstract: Willy Joseph Cancel Jr., 22, died in combat earlier this week, according to his uncle. He is believed to be the first American killed while fighting in Ukraine.

2022-04-30.title: Civilians continue to seek refuge at a besieged steel complex in Mariupol.
2022-04-30.abstract: That citizens are still seeking to shelter at the plant, where there is no prospect of escape or rescue, offers a testament to what they must be facing elsewhere in the devastated city.

2022-04-30.title: Christian Village in Israel Digs Into Its Crusader Past
2022-04-30.abstract: The residents of Mi’ilya long wondered what archaeological treasures lay beneath a crumbling castle. Now, some are excavating their homes to find out.

2022-04-30.title: A Winnipeg Landmark Rich in Symbolism Comes Under Indigenous Control
2022-04-30.abstract: The Hudson’s Bay Company has turned over its former flagship store to an Indigenous group with big ambitions for the space.

2022-04-30.title: Sunflower Oil ‘Vanishes’ as Ukraine War Grinds On
2022-04-30.abstract: Several British supermarkets have joined other chains around the world in asking shoppers to limit their cooking oil purchases, as supplies dwindle and prices rise.
